economic_notes: Approximately 44 homes under construction or substantially complete under CMHC Rapid Housing Initiative. Inland container port (Scotiaport Inc.) in planning/development stage. Wind turbine partnership (Truro Millbrook Wind Limited Partnership 51%). Hotel partnership (We'Kopekwitk Service Hotel Limited Partnership 97%). Retail tobacco, gaming, fisheries, aquaculture, and rental operations.
notes: Comparative 2023 figures restated due to asset retirement obligations not previously recognized (Note 12) - $934,000 liability added. Three government business enterprises accounted for by modified equity method: We'Kopekwitk Service Hotel LP (97%), Truro Millbrook Wind LP (51%), Scotiaport Inc. (50%). 18 incorporated/unincorporated entities fully consolidated. Large CMHC deferred revenue balance of $4,276,897 related to Housing Accelerator Fund and National Housing Co-Investment Fund. Significant increase in deferred revenue from $318,045 to $4,537,263. CMHC Rapid Housing Initiative revenue of $4,398,750 recognized. Total government transfers calculated as ISC + METS + CMHC + Additional government funding = 21,934,764 + 651,088 + 413,092 + 6,361,261. Schedule of remuneration not included in provided text.
